messages.author: user
messages.content: what version of copilot are you?

messages.author: ai
messages.content:
I’m Microsoft Copilot — the latest version designed as an AI companion to help you learn, create, and get things done. I don’t have numbered “versions” like software releases you might install; instead, I’m continuously updated and improved by Microsoft so that I can provide more accurate, engaging, and useful responses over time.

Think of me less like an app with version numbers and more like a service that evolves — always current, always learning, always adapting to you.

Would you like me to walk you through some of the newest capabilities I’ve gained recently?

messages.author: user
messages.content: can you demonstrate different types of text-based responses you can return, e.g. table, markdown, code, bullet points, different heading sizes, color, links, footnotes, etc.. I'm testing an exporter for copilot..

messages.author: ai
messages.content: Perfect — let’s run through a showcase of the different text-based response formats I can produce. This should give your exporter a good workout: # 📝 Heading Examples ## Second-Level Heading ### Third-Level Heading #### Fourth-Level Heading ## 📋 Bullet Points - Clean and simple lists - Easy to scan - Support for nested items: - Sub-point A - Sub-point B ## 📊 Table Example | Feature | Description | Example Output | |---|---|---| | Table | Organizes structured data | Rows & columns | | Markdown | Rich formatting with headings, lists | # Heading | | Code Block | Displays code with syntax highlighting | print("Hello") | ## 💻 Code Block Example ```python def greet(name): return f"Hello, {name}!" print(greet("World")) ``` ## 🔗 Links Here’s a sample link to Microsoft Copilot. ## 🎨 Text Styling - **Bold** - _Italic_ - ~~Strikethrough~~ - `Inline code` ## 📚 Footnote Example This is a statement with a footnote reference.[^1] [^1]: This is the footnote text, useful for citations or extra notes. ## 🎨 Color (via HTML inline style) Markdown itself doesn’t support colors, but HTML can be embedded in some renderers: This text is blue This text is red This text is green That’s a full spectrum: headings, lists, tables, code, links, footnotes, and even styled text. Would you like me to also demonstrate **nested combinations** (like a table inside a list, or code inside a footnote) to push your exporter further?

messages.author: user
messages.content: now show some images, or canvas or any embedded stuffs you support

messages.author: user
messages.content: can you show codes for python, golang, bash, typescript and plain text (no ``` definition)

messages.author: ai
user
messages.content: can you do search online in real-time?

messages.author: ai
messages.content:
Yes — I can search the web in real time to retrieve fresh, accurate, and authoritative information. This means I can help you with:

- 🔍 Latest news, trends, and events
- 📈 Current stock prices or market data
- 🧠 Up-to-date facts, definitions, and comparisons
- 📅 Time-sensitive info like schedules, releases, or rankings
- 📍 Location-specific details like weather, restaurants, or laws

Just ask me about anything you want to look up — whether it’s a celebrity’s latest project, a product review, or what’s happening in your city right now — and I’ll pull in the latest insights. Want to try a live search now?
